key_insight: A sparse random projection plus multinomial logistic regression model improved CNS tumor classification from DNA methylation data, outperforming a prior reference approach.
clinical_relevance: More accurate tumor subtype assignment could support better-informed diagnostic classification and downstream treatment decisions in CNS oncology.
method: Sparse Random Projection used to reduce high-dimensional methylation features
Multinomial logistic regression used for multiclass tumor classification
Evaluated against a widely used reference classifier in the same experimental setting
evidence_signal: The model achieved 96% mean accuracy in stratified 3-fold cross-validation on 2,801 reference samples and 86% class-level accuracy, rising to 93% at the family level, on 1,104 independent clinical samples.
watch_item: Performance gains may not fully generalize across institutions, assay platforms, or real-world deployment settings without further external validation.
paper_title: A Novel Machine Learning Approach for Central Nervous System Tumor Classification from DNA Methylation
